methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Helios is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Jonathan Geifman and Dor Blum that has raised $26M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage private companies (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M) alongside one public comparable and a broader private comp universe of eight additional frontier-energy businesses.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses total capital raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage advancement, yielding $52M. The peer-median method anchors to the median valuation of the three identified comparables at $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. These methods produced meaningfully different implications, reflecting the gap between early-stage capital intensity and peer trading multiples in this sector.

Triangulating both approaches yields a valuation range of $44M (low) to $172M (high), with a median implied value of $101M. The dispersion across methods—a 3.9x span from low to high—reflects material uncertainty around whether Helios's capital efficiency and competitive positioning align with the lower end of the peer distribution or command pricing closer to sector leaders. Key unknowns include technology maturity, path to revenue, and relative progress against the development milestones implicit in peer valuations.
